creator: thefloridaqueenie
caption:
Comment “JW” and I’ll send you a link to check out this gorgeous resort!

Before we dive in be sure to follow @thefloridaqueenie for more amazing stays in Florida! 

I just spent the dreamiest weekend at the JW Marriott Orlando Grande Lakes and I’m officially obsessed!

Think: luxe vibes, epic waterpark with a quarter mile long lazy river, water slides for the kids, (and adults who like to act like kids ), peaceful lagoons to relax in, and poolside restaurants and bars. 

From relaxing lagoons to Michelin-star meals poolside—this resort is a total gem just minutes from the theme parks!

Checkout out my Orlando Highlight for more details on it and follow me for more great places to stay in Florida! 

#JWMarriottOrlando #orlandoflorida #orlandovacation #floridaresort #marriott

transcription.text: If you're looking for an amazing resort in Orlando that's minutes from all the theme parks, but is also luxurious, then you need to check out the JW Marriott Orlando Grand Lakes. I just got back from an incredible weekend here, so come check it out with me. The focal point of the resort is the water park, which consists of three pools, relaxing lagoons, and a quarter mile long lazy river that winds through waterfalls and tropical gardens. There are also three water slides if you're feeling more adventurous or have kids in tow. This will keep them busy for hours. I checked into a double queen room with views of the lake, water park, and golf course. The room was very spacious and I loved the marble bathroom with a separate shower and tub. I even got a front row seat to the resort's firework show from the comfort of my own bed. During my stay, I dined at Primo, which is their authentic Italian restaurant. I recommend the meatball appetizer and the lobster pasta entree. I also dined at Whisper Creek Kitchen, which is their farm-to-table restaurant with a southern flair. I recommend the mahi tacos or their burger. I also spent a day by the pool in the Oasis Grand Cabana. It has plenty of comfortable seating, a tv, its own private hot tub, and a refrigerator stocked with drinks. While at the pool, I ordered some lunch from Knife Burger, run by a Michelin star chef nonetheless. I highly recommend the ahi tuna poke bowl and the hummus platter. Both were so fresh. This has been my favorite resort in Orlando thus far and if you want to stay here too, comment JW and I'll send you all the info. Send this to someone you want to stay here with.
